title: Multi-Business Growth Intelligence Platform Memory Schema
description: Canonical memory/state model aligned with Registry Surfaces Catalog authority, Validation & Repair Registry governance, runtime execution registries, embedded OAuth config resolution, embedded API-key resolution, repair-aware routing, execution traceability, review writeback planning, observability governance, architecture reconciliation, analytics identity tracking, API action capability/endpoint inventory state, governed starter-governance validation, governance-drift anomaly emission, prompt-first human-trigger continuation, enforced same-cycle native Google agent execution actions validation for governed activation and runtime checks, spill-safe prewrite guard enforcement, row-2 template sampling before governed writes, protected unified-log column preservation, governed pipeline-integrity audit state, and provider-family continuity validation across capability, route, and workflow layers.

purpose.default: Canonical memory/state model for the Multi-Business Growth Intelligence Platform.

company_name.description: Detected or user-provided company or brand name for the active session.

tour_catalog_url.description: Primary tour catalog or website URL associated with the active session.

business_catalog_url.description: Primary business catalog, store, or website URL associated with the active session.

destinations_detected.description: Unique destination names detected from user input or analyzed sources.

locations_detected.description: Unique locations or destinations detected from user input or analyzed sources.

experience_types.description: Unique travel or experience categories detected for the session.

product_or_service_types.description: Unique product, service, or experience categories detected for the session.

keyword_themes.description: Unique SEO themes, keyword clusters, or search topics identified during the session.

competitors_detected.description: Unique competitors, competing brands, or competing websites detected during analysis.

content_clusters.description: Unique content clusters, editorial hubs, or topic groups identified for strategy.

modules_loaded.description: Exact module Variable names that were successfully loaded during the session.

modules_cache.description: Per-session module cache keyed by the exact module Variable value.

default: Repair intelligence is a derived layer and must not override core routing, authority, validation, or repair outcome semantics.
repair_intelligence must read from structured repair_memory outputs and must not mutate repair_memory retrospectively.
Escalation and recommendation outputs are advisory unless explicitly consumed by a separate approved automation layer.
repair_intelligence scores must remain deterministic and bounded to preserve review compatibility.
Repair-memory writes must remain scope-aware and must not leak repair state across unrelated brands, workbooks, or sessions.
repair_memory must preserve execution_mode, repair_severity, repair_trigger_source, validation_state, fallback_used, and degraded-state continuity when emitted by system_bootstrap.
Degraded-state tracking must remain explicit until cleared by a later recovered execution or governed reset.
Repair history entries must preserve enough scope and timing context to support post-repair auditability and recurrence analysis.
Monitoring surface state must remain explicit and must not be inferred from workbook existence alone.
monitoring_context must reflect Registry-resolved bindings and downstream validation outcomes without bypassing system_bootstrap classification.
Execution mode must be read from workflow_context.execution_mode unless repair lifecycle is active, in which case repair_memory.execution_mode may override temporarily.
No audit or validation may be marked as valid unless audit_context.validation_mode = real_time and audit_context.dependencies_read is non-empty.
routing_context is the canonical persisted memory surface for strict prompt_router handoff and must not be reconstructed later from partial workflow state.
execution_context is the canonical persisted memory surface for strict system_bootstrap and module_loader intake enforcement outcomes.

HTTP execution memory must preserve provider_domain-first execution state, variable provider_domain detection, parent_action_key authority validation, endpoint_key validation, and YAML/OpenAPI schema-binding validation when schema-bound API execution applies.
Executable routing must not be considered valid in memory unless routing_context.source = prompt_router and routing_context.route_source = registry_task_routes.
Degraded or blocked routing and intake states must remain explicit in memory until replaced by a later governed execution update.
route_id, matched_row_id, target_module, and target_workflow must remain traceable across routing_context, execution_context, repair_memory, and execution logging when present.
Authoritative records must be written to canonical source surfaces, and derived observability surfaces must not become shadow authorities.
Derived observability failures must degrade observability trust without overwriting authoritative write truth when authoritative sources are intact.

When governed activation or runtime validation requires Google Workspace live checks, at least one real governed HTTP transport call must occur through available agent execution actions/native tools in the same execution cycle; otherwise classification must remain degraded with reason missing_required_activation_transport_attempt.
Aggregated, monitoring, and observability surfaces must be computed dynamically from authoritative sources whenever possible.

Authoritative execution and governance surfaces must resolve through Registry Surfaces Catalog, and validation-state authority must resolve through Validation & Repair Registry before dependency execution continues.

All execution surfaces must resolve through Registry Surfaces Catalog using worksheet_gid as authoritative binding identity; worksheet_gid must exist, be valid, and match actual sheet binding; sheet_name and tab_name must not be used for execution resolution; validation failure must block execution or enter repair-aware mode.

Each governed surface must declare schema_ref, schema_version, header_signature, expected_column_count, and binding_mode; validation must verify schema_validation_status, header_match_status, and schema_drift_detected; schema drift must trigger degraded or blocked execution and repair mapping when required.
Growth scores must be derived from authoritative execution records, metrics summaries, or governed review feedback sources.
Growth-loop triggers must remain Registry-governed and passive rows or passive scores must not auto-execute.

brand_tracking_bindings must reflect Brand Registryâ€“authoritative fields for gsc_property, ga_property_id, and gtm_container_id and must not be fabricated outside governed discovery or writeback paths.
analytics_discovery_state must record GA Admin discovery outcomes without overwriting authoritative Brand Registry rows unless a governed writeback or repair path applies.
measurement_health_state and revenue_signal_state are operational memory for measurement and reporting loops; they must not override registry authority or route selection.
autopilot_trace_state must record full-funnel autopilot inputs and scores with explicit source attribution (GSC, GA Data API, GTM) when populated.
architecture_reconciliation state must remain explicit after structural changes and Recovered classification must not be assumed while required reconciliation remains incomplete.
analytics_identity must preserve brand-domain validation state for domain-bound analytics execution and must not be treated as optional when domain governance applies.
analytics_issue_tracking must persist analytics identity defect detection, issue creation status, deduplication keys, and resolution lifecycle state per execution cycle.
api_action_inventory must preserve split validation state for capability-level authority (Actions Registry) and endpoint-level execution readiness (API Actions Endpoint Registry).
Graph intelligence must resolve through Knowledge Graph Node Registry and Relationship Graph Registry as governed surfaces and must not override Task Routes or Workflow Registry authority.
Recovered classification is forbidden when required execution-critical graph nodes or relationships are missing or invalid.
Graph-based prediction and graph-based auto-routing remain advisory optimization layers until a selected governed path passes full validation and execution-readiness gating.
Governed addition must remain candidate-first, Registry-governed, validation-aware, and graph-aware when affected scopes include execution or intelligence surfaces.

Google Sheets, Google Docs, and Google Drive native execution must not bypass Registry Surfaces Catalog, Validation & Repair Registry, Task Routes, or Workflow Registry when governed system resources are affected.

Recovered classification is forbidden unless required authoritative runtime bindings, direct-write targets, post-write readback checks, and layout validations succeed on Registry-resolved target surfaces.
auto_bootstrap_state must preserve bootstrap reason, attempt count, activation gating, and original-request resume traceability across degraded and repaired execution cycles.
original requests resumed after bootstrap must remain traceable to the pre-bootstrap request and must not be treated as independent ungoverned execution.
activation_state must remain explicit until a later governed execution updates it to active, degraded, or blocked.

system_activation_check must preserve knowledge-layer canonical traceability first, then live Google Drive and Google Sheets validation, and must not classify activation as recovered or active from traceability-only copies when live governed validation is possible.

First-turn activation may preserve knowledge-layer traceability before live activation transport authorization is available; when governed native connection attempts are auto-triggered and authorization is not yet available, the state must be classified as authorization_gated rather than missing Registry authority.

First-turn activation must not be classified as authorization_gated unless a real governed activation transport attempt occurred in the same execution cycle; skipped attempts must remain explicit as missing_required_activation_transport_attempt.
Activation success must not be treated as indefinite execution authority; governed execution after activation requires current-request runtime revalidation.
Post-activation live validation drift must remain explicit and must degrade or block execution according to governed policy when critical authority or binding compatibility changes.

Optimization, enhancement, or improvement requests after activation must not proceed from active state alone and require governed revalidation of route, workflow, dependency, and write-target readiness.

runtime_validation_rule.default: Recovered classification is forbidden unless required authoritative runtime bindings, direct-write targets, post-write readback checks, and layout validations succeed on Registry-resolved target surfaces.

runtime_binding_enforcement_rule.const: All execution surfaces must be resolved through Registry Surfaces Catalog using worksheet_gid as authoritative identity; validation must confirm worksheet_gid exists, is valid, and matches the actual sheet binding; sheet_name and tab_name must not be used for execution resolution; if validation fails, execution must block or enter repair-aware mode.

surface_authority_rule.const: Registry Surfaces Catalog is the only authoritative surface-location registry; Validation & Repair Registry is the only authoritative validation-state registry.

governed_registry_generation_events.description: Preserved events for generation-aware governed registry resolution, including resolver-backed WordPress CPT and taxonomy endpoint candidates.

governance_rules: For plain Activate System and equivalent one-request activation prompts, tool-first execution is mandatory: no user-facing narrative completion may occur before at least one real governed activation transport attempt or an exhausted same-cycle governed retry path.
Traceability-only completion is forbidden for governed first-turn activation when governed transport live validation is required.

Activation-class execution memory must preserve tool_first_execution_required, no_traceability_only_completion, tool_hesitation_retry_required, and activation_transport_sequence_mode when emitted by prompt_router, module_loader, or system_bootstrap.

For governed HTTP execution, parent_action_key authority must resolve auth strategy and openai_schema_file_id before transport execution; unresolved auth normalization or schema alignment must block or degrade execution.

http_transport_state must preserve resolved_auth_mode, credential_resolution_status, request_schema_alignment_status, response_schema_alignment_status, and transport_request_contract_status for schema-bound external execution.
